all.en: Ever since I was a teenager, I have dreamed of playing the guitar in front of my friends.
all.zh: 自從我還是青少年時，就一直夢想著能在朋友面前彈吉他。

all.en: However, formal lessons at the music school turned out to be too expensive for my budget.
all.zh: 然而，音樂學校的正式課程對我的預算來說實在太昂貴了。

all.en: Last month, I finally decided to teach myself by watching free tutorials on YouTube every evening.
all.zh: 上個月，我終於決定每天晚上透過觀看 YouTube 上的免費教學影片自學。

all.en: At first, my fingers hurt badly because the strings dug into my soft skin.
all.zh: 一開始，因為琴弦會陷入我柔軟的皮膚，我的手指痛得厲害。

all.en: I struggled to switch between basic chords like G, C and D smoothly.
all.zh: 我很難在 G、C 和 D 等基本和弦之間流暢地轉換。

all.en: A friendly creator named Marty Music explained each step slowly and clearly.
all.zh: 一位名叫 Marty Music 的友善創作者把每個步驟都解釋得既緩慢又清楚。

all.en: After two weeks of daily practice, I was able to strum a simple pop song from beginning to end.
all.zh: 經過兩週的每日練習後，我能夠從頭到尾彈完一首簡單的流行歌曲。

all.en: My neighbor knocked on the door once to complain about the noise late at night.
all.zh: 我的鄰居曾經敲門抱怨深夜的噪音。

all.en: Since then, I have been wearing headphones connected to an electric guitar for quiet practice.
all.zh: 從那時起，我就一直戴著耳機接電吉他做安靜的練習。

all.en: Recording my progress on a phone helps me notice mistakes I cannot hear while playing.
all.zh: 用手機錄下我的進度，能幫助我察覺彈奏時聽不出來的錯誤。

all.en: Next month, I plan to perform a song at my younger sister's birthday party.
all.zh: 下個月，我計畫在妹妹的生日派對上表演一首歌。

all.en: This experience has taught me that patience and consistency are more important than expensive lessons.
all.zh: 這個經驗教會我，耐心和持之以恆比昂貴的課程更為重要。

all.en: On the night of the birthday party, my hands shook slightly as I tuned each string.
all.zh: 在生日派對當晚，我調整每根琴弦時雙手微微顫抖。

all.en: My sister gathered everyone in the living room and turned off the bright ceiling lights.
all.zh: 妹妹把大家聚集到客廳，並關掉了明亮的天花板燈。

all.en: I sat on a wooden stool and dedicated the song to her eighteenth birthday.
all.zh: 我坐在一張木凳上，把這首歌獻給她的十八歲生日。

all.en: Halfway through the chorus, my voice cracked, but nobody seemed to mind at all.
all.zh: 唱到副歌一半時，我的聲音破音了，但似乎沒有人在意。

all.en: My sister hugged me tightly and admitted she had never expected this surprise.
all.zh: 妹妹緊緊地抱住我，承認她從未預料到會有這個驚喜。

all.en: My parents quietly wiped away tears in the back of the crowded room.
all.zh: 我的父母在擁擠房間的後方默默擦著眼淚。

all.en: Several relatives recorded videos and shared them on their social media accounts.
all.zh: 幾位親戚錄下了影片，並分享到他們的社群媒體帳號上。

all.en: A cousin requested an encore, so I played another simple song I had been practicing.
all.zh: 一位表親要求再唱一首，所以我又彈了另一首我一直在練習的簡單歌曲。

all.en: Strangers online began commenting kindly after she posted the clip the next morning.
all.zh: 妹妹隔天早上把片段貼出來後，網路上的陌生人開始留下親切的留言。

all.en: That single performance gave me the courage to continue learning new songs every week.
all.zh: 那一場表演給了我繼續每週學習新歌的勇氣。

all.en: Over the following weeks, I expanded my repertoire to include slow ballads and gentle folk songs.
all.zh: 在接下來的幾週裡，我把曲目擴展到了慢板抒情歌曲和輕柔的民謠。

all.en: My fingers gradually built calluses, and chord changes started to feel natural and automatic.
all.zh: 我的手指逐漸長出了繭，和弦變換開始感覺自然又自動。

all.en: A local café owner invited me to perform a short acoustic set on a quiet Friday evening.
all.zh: 一位在地咖啡店老闆邀請我在一個寧靜的週五晚上演出一場簡短的木吉他表演。

all.en: Before the show, I rehearsed the same five songs more than a hundred times at home.
all.zh: 演出之前，我在家裡反覆排練了同樣的五首歌超過一百次。

all.en: On the night of the performance, the small audience sat close and listened with full attention.
all.zh: 演出當晚，小小的觀眾群坐得很近，全神貫注地聆聽。

all.en: A woman in the front row requested a song I had only just begun learning that week.
all.zh: 前排的一位女士點了一首我那一週才剛開始學的歌。

all.en: I bravely attempted the song and laughed through a few mistakes that nobody seemed to mind.
all.zh: 我勇敢地嘗試了那首歌，並在幾個沒人在意的錯誤中笑著彈完。

all.en: After the show, the owner offered me a regular monthly slot at his cozy little venue.
all.zh: 演出結束後，老闆提供我在他舒適小場地每月固定一場的演出機會。

all.en: I started a small YouTube channel myself to document my journey from absolute beginner to performer.
all.zh: 我自己開了一個小型的 YouTube 頻道，用來記錄我從完全初學者到表演者的歷程。

all.en: Several viewers thanked me for showing that self-taught musicians can still reach a real stage.
all.zh: 好幾位觀眾感謝我證明了自學的音樂人也能登上真正的舞台。

all.en: My YouTube channel slowly attracted a small but loyal group of self-taught music enthusiasts worldwide.
all.zh: 我的 YouTube 頻道慢慢吸引了一小群忠實的自學音樂愛好者，遍布世界各地。

all.en: A viewer from Japan sent me a kind email asking permission to translate my video subtitles.
all.zh: 一位來自日本的觀眾寄來一封親切的電子郵件，請求我授權翻譯我的影片字幕。

all.en: I happily agreed, and soon Japanese, Korean and English subtitles appeared on my most popular videos.
all.zh: 我欣然同意，很快地，日文、韓文和英文字幕便出現在我最熱門的影片上。

all.en: The monthly performances at the cafe became increasingly crowded, sometimes filling every single available seat.
all.zh: 咖啡館每月一次的演出愈來愈擁擠，有時座無虛席。

all.en: The owner asked if I would consider hosting an open mic night for other self-taught musicians.
all.zh: 老闆問我是否願意主持一個讓其他自學音樂人上台演出的開放麥之夜。

all.en: On the first open mic night, six nervous performers showed up with stories similar to mine.
all.zh: 在第一場開放麥之夜，六位緊張的表演者帶著與我相似的故事登台。

all.en: We supported each other warmly, and several formed friendships that continued long after the event.
all.zh: 我們溫暖地互相支持，好幾位在活動結束後仍維持著長久的友誼。

all.en: I began offering free online lessons on weekends for beginners who could not afford private teachers.
all.zh: 我開始在週末為負擔不起私人老師費用的初學者提供免費的線上課程。

all.en: One of my students recently performed her own song at the cafe and thanked me publicly onstage.
all.zh: 我的一位學生最近在咖啡館演出了她自己創作的歌，並在台上公開向我道謝。

all.en: Music, once just a lonely hobby, had grown into a small community that supported each other beautifully.
all.zh: 音樂，從前只是孤單的興趣，已經發展成一個彼此美好支持的小型社群。

all.en: After two years of teaching online, my channel grew to over fifty thousand loyal subscribers worldwide.
all.zh: 線上教學兩年後，我的頻道成長到擁有超過五萬位來自世界各地的忠實訂閱者。

all.en: A small Taiwanese guitar brand reached out and offered to sponsor my next series of beginner lessons.
all.zh: 一家小型的台灣吉他品牌主動聯繫，提議贊助我下一系列的初學者課程。

all.en: They sent me three carefully crafted guitars to test, review and recommend to my growing student community.
all.zh: 他們寄來三把精心製作的吉他，供我測試、評論並推薦給我日益成長的學生社群。

all.en: I designed a new course focused specifically on shy adult learners who had given up music years ago.
all.zh: 我設計了一套新課程，特別針對多年前曾放棄音樂的害羞成人學習者。

all.en: Hundreds of students enrolled, including office workers, retired engineers and even one quiet hospital nurse.
all.zh: 數百位學生報名，包括上班族、退休工程師，甚至還有一位安靜的醫院護理師。

all.en: The nurse later told me that playing guitar helped her cope with long, emotionally exhausting hospital shifts.
all.zh: 那位護理師後來告訴我，彈吉他幫助她度過漫長且情緒上極為疲憊的醫院班次。

all.en: My cafe owner suggested hosting a yearly community concert featuring only my self-taught students on stage.
all.zh: 我的咖啡店老闆建議舉辦一年一度的社區音樂會，舞台上只邀請我這些自學的學生表演。

all.en: On the first concert night, twelve students performed bravely while their families recorded every single moment proudly.
all.zh: 在第一場音樂會當晚，十二位學生勇敢上台演出，他們的家人自豪地錄下了每一個瞬間。

all.en: Watching them play reminded me of my own trembling hands at my sister's birthday party years earlier.
all.zh: 看著他們演奏，讓我想起了多年前在妹妹生日派對上自己顫抖的雙手。

all.en: Music had truly become a quiet thread connecting strangers, families and unexpected friendships across the entire community.
all.zh: 音樂真正成了一條安靜的線，連結著整個社區中的陌生人、家庭與意想不到的友誼。

all.en: The young nurse asked if she could organize a small concert at her hospital pediatric ward.
all.zh: 那位年輕護理師詢問，是否能在她任職醫院的兒科病房舉辦一場小型音樂會。

all.en: I agreed, and six of my older students volunteered to play simple gentle songs for the children.
all.zh: 我答應了，六位我較年長的學生主動報名，為孩子們演奏簡單溫柔的曲子。

all.en: We tuned our guitars in a small staff break room filled with quiet, hopeful afternoon light.
all.zh: 我們在一個充滿安靜午後希望陽光的小型員工休息室裡，調好了吉他。

all.en: The children gathered in pajamas, some holding tubes, others holding worn stuffed animals close.
all.zh: 孩子們穿著睡衣聚集過來，有的手上接著點滴管，有的緊緊抱著破舊的絨毛玩偶。

all.en: We played four soft songs, ending with a lullaby the nurse had quietly taught her young patients.
all.zh: 我們演奏了四首柔和的曲子，最後以那位護理師教過小病人的搖籃曲作結。

all.en: Many parents wiped tears quietly while staff joined in humming along from the doorway corners.
all.zh: 許多父母靜靜地擦著眼淚，員工們則從門邊的角落跟著輕輕哼唱。

all.en: Afterward, one tiny patient asked shyly if she could try strumming my smallest student guitar.
all.zh: 演奏後，一位小小的病童害羞地問，能不能試著彈一下我那把最小的學生吉他。

all.en: I knelt down, helped her hold a soft chord, and watched her face light up with wonder.
all.zh: 我蹲下來，幫她按住一個柔和的和弦，看著她臉上綻放出驚奇的光彩。

all.en: Her mother whispered that it was the first true smile her daughter had given in many weeks.
all.zh: 她的母親輕聲說，那是她女兒這幾週以來真正展露的第一個笑容。

all.en: A free YouTube lesson had quietly carried music into rooms I could never have imagined visiting.
all.zh: 一堂免費的 YouTube 教學，已悄悄把音樂帶進了我從未想像能造訪的房間。
